Off Calendar Jan-31-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Edward Koplowitz for George Poppic. Andy Zabronsky for Wells Fargo Bank.

The Petition for Relief From Breach of Trust, etc. and the Petition for Return of Distributed Property was referred to the department of the Presiding Judge (206) to be set for trial, (order for the same signed in open court).

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Edward Koplowitz for The Poppic Petitioners. Andrew Zabronsky for Wells Fargo.

The matter was continued to January 31, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Mr. Koplowitz to review the information received from Wells Fargo.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy W. Weatherly

Petition For Relief From Breach Of Trust; For Indemnification; For Declaration Of Rights; For Surcharge
Off Calendar Jan-03-2012 Continued To Jan-31-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Relief From Breach Of Trust; For Indemnification; For Declaration Of Rights; For Surcharge
Off Calendar Nov-15-2011 Continued To Jan-03-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Andrew Zabronsky for Wells Fargo Bank. Edward Koplowitz for the Poppic Petitioners.

The matters were continued to January 3, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Mr. Koplowitz and his Clients to review the report and decide what they are going to do.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy A. Whitehead.

Petition For Return Of Distributed Property
Off Calendar Nov-15-2011 Continued To Jan-03-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Relief From Breach Of Trust; For Indemnification; For Declaration Of Rights; For Surcharge
Off Calendar Nov-01-2011 Continued To Nov-15-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Return Of Distributed Property
Off Calendar Nov-01-2011 Continued To Nov-15-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Relief From Breach Of Trust; For Indemnification; For Declaration Of Rights; For Surcharge
Off Calendar Sep-13-2011 Continued To Nov-01-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ed Koplowitz for Petitioners. Andrew Zabronsky for Wells Fargo Bank.

Both matters were continued to November 1, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Zabronsky agreed to provide a report to Mr. Koplowitz with as much information as possible by September 30, 2011.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy C. Garcia.

Petition For Return Of Distributed Property
Off Calendar Sep-13-2011 Continued To Nov-01-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Relief From Breach Of Trust; For Indemnification; For Declaration Of Rights; For Surcharge
Off Calendar Jul-27-2011 Continued To Sep-13-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ed Koplowitz for George Poppic. Andrew Zabronsky for Wells Fargo Bank.

Mr. Zabronsky stated that the environmental litigation was resloved and that they were memorializing the last of three settlement agreements. In addition, Mr. Zabronsky stated that he discussed with Mr. Koplowitz a proposal that within 30-days (around September 1st) Wells Fargo will provide a report capturing the costs to date and anticipated costs going forward, the settlement proceeds and the contributions from the insurance company so that they can see where they are and decide if they can resolve their matter themselves or go forward with litigation. The matter was continued to September 13, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of trial setting, what remediation is left and costs.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Cabrera.

Petition For Return Of Distributed Property
Off Calendar Jul-27-2011 Continued To Sep-13-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Relief From Breach Of Trust; For Indemnification; For Declaration Of Rights; For Surcharge
Off Calendar Jun-01-2011 Continued To Jul-27-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Claudia Lowder for Wells Fargo (Trustee). Edward Koplowitz for Poppic Petitioners.

Mr. Koplowitz stated that he did receive a May report from Mr. Zabronsky, that unfortunately that Mr. Zabronsky could not be here today, that he was informed by Ms. Lowder this morning that the trial date on the environmental issue is still set, and that there is a settlement conference set for June 10. Ms. Lowder stated that there was a settlement with the manufacturer for $130,000.00, that she did not have any information in regard to the monitoring of the property, and that the trial date is set for June 23, 2011. Discussions were held in regard to continuing this matter past June 23, 2011. At the conclusion of discussions, the Court continued the matter to July 27, 2011 for status on the environmental litigation.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Petition For Return Of Distributed Property
Off Calendar Jun-01-2011 Continued To Jul-27-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Relief From Breach Of Trust; For Indemnification; For Declaration Of Rights; For Surcharge
Off Calendar Apr-05-2011 Continued To Jun-01-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Andrew Zabronski for Wells Fargo Bank. Edward Koplowitz for Petitioners Poppic.

Mr. Zabronsky stated that the trial date of June 20th on the environmental issue is still holding, that mediation was unsuccessful, that a Summary Judgment Motion will be heard this month and that expert discovery was currently going on. Mr. Koplowitz stated that he did not receive a report this month, that he did not know what was happening with regard to remediation of the properties and that he would like an update within the next two weeks. Mr. Zabronsky also stated that two of the three properties were leased out, that the other property was on the market for leasing and that they were still waiting for a response from the City with regard to the proposal made to put the property in a monitoring phase. The matter was continued to June 1, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for further status.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Whitehead.

Petition For Return Of Distributed Property
Off Calendar Apr-05-2011 Continued To Jun-01-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Relief From Breach Of Trust; For Indemnification; For Declaration Of Rights; For Surcharge
Off Calendar Apr-01-2011 Continued To Apr-05-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Return Of Distributed Property
Off Calendar Apr-01-2011 Continued To Apr-05-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Relief From Breach Of Trust; For Indemnification; For Declaration Of Rights; For Surcharge
Off Calendar Jan-25-2011 Continued To Apr-01-11 At 08:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Andrew Zabronsky for Wells Fargo Bank (Trustee). Ed Koplowitz for George Poppic.

Mr. Zabronsky stated that the June trial date is being held, that the discovery cut off date is late next month and that mediation was continued to February 10, 2011. Mr. Zabronsky also spoke of the environmental issue. The matter was continued to April 1, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation on the environmental issue.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy B. Bloom.

Petition For Return Of Distributed Property
Off Calendar Jan-25-2011 Continued To Apr-01-11 At 08:30 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Relief From Breach Of Trust; For Indemnification; For Declaration Of Rights; For Surcharge
Off Calendar Nov-30-2010 Continued To Jan-25-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ed Koplowitz for Poppic Petitioners. Andrew Zabronsky for Wells Fargo Bank.

Mr. Zabronsky stated that mediation occurred, that there was no reliable estimate of the total costs of remediation, that mediation was re-scheduled for the second week in January, and that Judge Armstrong was keeping the June 20th trial date. In addition, Mr. Zabronsky stated that the Regulators do not have a prepared action plan with regard to the environmental issue. Mr. Koplowitz raised the issue of the reports being sporadic, stated that he has heard the same at the last hearings and spoke of his Clients frustration. The Court set a target date of the first of every month for the report to be provided to Mr. Koplowitz. Mr. Zabronsky stated that there is liability coverage, that there was an agreement with the carrier to cover legal costs and the environmental litigation, and that an expert disclosure was due next month. The matter was continued to January 25, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Petition For Relief From Breach Of Trust; For Indemnification; For Declaration Of Rights; For Surcharge
Off Calendar Sep-21-2010 Continued To Nov-16-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Andrew Zabronsky for Wells Fargo Bank (Trustee). Ed Koplowitz for Petitioners.

Mr. Zabronsky reported that the environmental litigation had its first mediation session scheduled for Thursday, that the Federal Judge signaled that the trial date in June of next year is a firm date, that settlement would need to take place expeditiously, that they were waiting for a report that's due in mid-June with an action plan stating firm numbers of what the costs are and that he was in favor of trailing this matter for those Guys to figure out what the damages are, if any. Mr. Koplowitz stated that he was unhappy with Wells Fargo entering into a contract to sell one of the properties without asking/telling them, requested the matter be sent out to the Department of the Presiding Judge for trial setting, stated that he did not want to wait anymore and that his Client was not part of the mediation. Discussion were held with regard to why Mr. Koplowitz and his Client should/should not be part of the mediation, whether the matter should be sent out for trial at this time, the need to conduct discovery and Wells Fargo suspending their fees. The matter was continued to November 16, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status mediation and for referral to Dept. 206 for trial setting. Counsel will discuss the issue of mediation.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Petition For Return Of Distributed Property
Off Calendar Sep-21-2010 Continued To Nov-16-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Relief From Breach Of Trust; For Indemnification; For Declaration Of Rights; For Surcharge
Off Calendar Apr-13-2010 Continued To Sep-21-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Edward Koplowitz for George Poppic. Andrew Zabronsky for Wells Fargo Bank.

Mr. Zabronsky stated that the environmental litigation matter was proceeding, that there were new Parties in the matter, that mediation was to be scheduled this fall and asked to trail this matter. Mr. Koplowitz stated that they needed to know the outcome of the underlying litigation matter in order to know what the damages are, that he was not getting monthly reports, that he had only received one and that Wells Fargo had to provide them. Mr. Zabronsky stated that he would provide Mr. Koplowitz with the monthly reports regarding litigation and the incurring fees on the first of the month. The matter was continued to September 21, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status and setting.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ed Koplowitz for Geroge Poppic (Petitioner). Andrew Zabronski for Wells Fargo Bank.

Mr. Zabronsky stated that there was extensive litigation with regard to the ground water contamination issue, that mediation was scheduled on Monday to determine damages/exposure and the Trustee's responsiblities, and asked that this matter be continued to see the outcome of mediation. MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Andrew Zabronsky for Wells Fargo Bank. Ed Koplowitz for George Poppic, Et Al.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Andrew Zabronsky for Wells Fargo Bank (Trustee). Ed Koplowitz for George Poppic (Petitioner).

The Parties presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the return of the distributed property and reported status on the contamination issue.
